canonical_definition: The latent ability of a model to recognize the underlying purpose or intent of an input prompt, particularly in discerning malicious from benign requests.
papers: Language Representations Can be What Recommenders Need: Findings and Potentials
Understanding and Enhancing the Transferability of Jailbreaking Attacks

definitions.text: The latent capacity to perceive text-based user intentions and refine recommendation results accordingly.
definitions.paper: Language Representations Can be What Recommenders Need: Findings and Potentials

relationships.paper: Understanding and Enhancing the Transferability of Jailbreaking Attacks
relationships.evidence: By incorporating adversarial sequences, these attacks can redirect the source LLM’s focus away from malicious-intent tokens in the original input, thereby obstructing the model’s intent recognition and eliciting harmful responses. (Abstract)
